title: Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 11.3.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 11.3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.33: Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.33' running on index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host") — severity, plugin_name, host_fqdn, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host") earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_app_tier.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT tier
| where tier="payment_app"
| where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)
| where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")
| stats dc(plugin_id) as open_plugins, values(plugin_name) as sample_plugins by host_fqdn
| sort - open_plugins
description: Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vm and sourcetypes qualys:host, tenable:sc:vuln. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize severity strings across vendors. (2) Deduplicate by `plugin_id`+asset. (3) Sync with patch CAB weekly. (4) Export for ASV/internal scan evidence crosswalk. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:host`, `sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln`; Lookups: `pci_app_tier`, `pci_app_tier.csv`.
visualization: Table (hosts with counts), bar (open_plugins), treemap by business unit lookup.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for prioritizes open critical and high findings on cde application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with pci risk ranking. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize severity strings across vendors. (2) Deduplicate by `plugin_id`+asset. (3) Sync with patch CAB weekly. (4) Export for ASV/internal scan evidence crosswalk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host") earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_app_tier.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT tier
| where tier="payment_app"
| where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)
| where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")
| stats dc(plugin_id) as open_plugins, values(plugin_name) as sample_plugins by host_fqdn
| sort - open_plugins
```

Understanding this SPL

**Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 11.3.1)** — Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where tier="payment_app"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host_fqdn** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 11.3.1)** — Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (hosts with counts), bar (open_plugins), treemap by business unit lookup.
